commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated repair is needed after the latest commit; the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof before merge.; Cleared: The diff changes agent runner logic and tests only; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, install scripts, package metadata, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=1c3af74950d031b27d03e27fccde59b637d0ce39)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a terminal `runEmbeddedAgent` branch and helper that emit a user-visible timeout error payload when a timed-out turn would otherwise return no payload, plus regression coverage for delivered, silent-context, and compaction-timeout cases.

PR surface: Source +129, Tests +125. Total +254 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: a timed-out attempt during tool execution skips the prompt-timeout branch, gets no incomplete-turn text, and reaches the final terminal return with no payloads on current main. I did not run a live 600s channel timeout in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed after the latest commit; the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): emit user-visible notice when a turn exhausts its time budget
target.body:
## Problem

When an agent turn exhausts its time budget (`agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`) or a run-level deadline **while no assistant reply has been produced**, the gateway can go completely silent — no message reaches the channel, no indication a limit was hit. From a user's seat this is indistinguishable from a hung process.

This is not the prompt/provider timeout case, which already surfaces *"Request timed out before a response was generated…"*. It's the timeout that fires **outside a model prompt** — most commonly while a tool is executing on a complex turn:

- `attempt.timedOut` is `true`, but because `countActiveToolExecutions(...) > 0` the runner also sets `timedOutDuringToolExecution = true`;
- `timedOutDuringPrompt = timedOut && !timedOutDuringCompaction && !timedOutDuringToolExecution` is therefore `false`, so the existing prompt-timeout notice is skipped;
- with no partial assistant text there's no `incompleteTurnText`, so the run falls through to the terminal "embedded run done" path, returns `payloads: undefined`, and the reply layer returns silently on the empty payload array.

## Fix

Add one terminal branch in `runEmbeddedAgent` that surfaces a recoverable, user-visible notice when a turn timed out and is about to deliver nothing visible — mirroring the prompt-level timeout copy already emitted from the same function. Because the branch returns a non-empty payload, it flows through the normal gateway-agnostic reply path (Discord, Matrix, etc.) and never reaches the silent return.

The decision is a small pure helper, `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice(...)`, returning the notice only when correct and `null` otherwise. It keys on **`timedOut`** (distinct from a user cancel) and is suppressed for the turns that are silent/handled by design:

- `timedOutDuringCompaction` — compaction timeouts are **paused/resumable**, not abandoned; emitting a notice (passed as `incompleteTurnText`) would also override the `paused` liveness state the terminal path preserves;
- `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` (the **raw** flag — note the derived `shouldTreatEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is always `false` for timed-out attempts, so it cannot protect this case) — cron/heartbeat/autonomous contexts;
- a messaging tool already delivered out-of-band (`hasMessagingToolDeliveryEvidence`);
- a deterministic approval prompt was delivered out-of-band (`didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt`; payload building intentionally suppresses assistant artifacts there).

A separate copy variant covers an idle (no-output) model timeout. Net change is additive: it only produces output where there is currently none.

## Proof

**1. Committed real-`runEmbeddedAgent` regression tests** (`run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`) drive the real terminal path via the existing harness loader:

- tool-execution timeout, empty reply → notice delivered, `livenessState: "abandoned"`;
- same, in a silent-reply context (`allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true`) → **no notice** (stays silent);
- compaction timeout → **no notice**, `livenessState: "paused"` preserved.

Plus full unit coverage of the helper's decision matrix. Suite: **123 passing**; `tsgo:core` and oxlint clean.

**2. Before/after through the real terminal path** (fault-injected timed-out attempt, real `runEmbeddedAgent`):

```
# tool-execution timeout, empty reply
BEFORE (origin/main):  result.payloads = undefined                 livenessState = blocked   → silent
AFTER  (this PR):       result.payloads = [{ text: "⚠️ I hit my time budget on this request and
                        stopped before finishing. Ask me to continue, or simplify the request.
                        If this happens often, raise `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` in your
                        config.", isError: true }]                  livenessState = abandoned → delivered

# compaction timeout, empty reply  (regression guard)
AFTER:                  result.payloads = undefined                 livenessState = paused    → correctly silent
```

**On a live gateway/channel capture:** the trigger is a ≥`agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` (default 600s) budget exhausted *during tool execution* — not reproducible against a real channel in CI or by an external contributor without a long-running live agent. The fault-injection harness drives the **real** `runEmbeddedAgent` terminal path (`makeAttemptResult({ timedOut: true, timedOutDuringToolExecution: true, assistantTexts: [] })`) and the committed tests assert end-to-end behavior through the same function; the harness itself is kept out of the committed diff to keep the patch narrow.
target.author: spencer2211

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
